response:
I first verified the message words W[0..15] against the given message and padding:
- W[0..11] match the successive 32-bit big-endian words of L0001 (checked W[0]=0x2F45795A at L0003, W[1]=0x5931492D at L0004, …, W[11]=0x6C2A2762 at L0014).
- W[12]=0x52258000 (L0015) reflects the 0x80 padding immediately after the last message byte 0x25, consistent with SHA-256 padding.
- W[13]=W[14]=0 (L0016–L0017).
- W[15]=0x00000190 (L0018) encodes the 400-bit message length (50 bytes × 8), which matches L0001.

Round 0 detailed small ops and sums:
- Inputs (L0069): a=0x6A09E667, b=0xBB67AE85, c=0x3C6EF372, d=0xA54FF53A, e=0x510E527F, f=0x9B05688C, g=0x1F83D9AB, h=0x5BE0CD19.
- S1 from e via rotates: ROTR^6(e)=0xFD443949, ROTR^11(e)=0x4FEA21CA, ROTR^25(e)=0x87293FA8, XOR ⇒ S1=0x3587272B (L0070), matching the line.
- ch = (e&f) xor (~e&g) = 0x1F85C98C (L0071), which I recomputed nibblewise from 0x510E527F, 0x9B05688C, 0x1F83D9AB.
- step1 = h + S1 = 0x5BE0CD19 + 0x3587272B = 0x9167F444 (L0072).
- step2 = step1 + ch = 0x9167F444 + 0x1F85C98C = 0xB0EDBDD0 (L0073).
- step3 = step2 + K[0] = 0xB0EDBDD0 + 0x428A2F98 = 0xF377ED68 (L0074).
- temp1 = step3 + W[0] = 0xF377ED68 + 0x2F45795A = 0x22BD66C2 (L0075).
- S0 from a and maj(a,b,c): maj = 0x3A6FE667 (L0077) verified bitwise; S0=0xCE2051FE (L0076); temp2 = S0 + maj = 0x08909AE5 (L0078).
- new a = temp1 + temp2 = 0x22BD66C2 + 0x08909AE5 = 0x2B4E01A7 (L0079).
- new e = d + temp1 = 0xA54FF53A + 0x22BD66C2 = 0xC80D5BFC (L0080).
- Variable shift check (L0081) matches the standard rotation of (a,b,c,d,e,f,g,h).

Round 1 (selected checks):
- step1 = h + S1 = 0x1F83D9AB + 0x8A14CAA0 = 0xA998A44B (L0086).
- step2 = step1 + ch = 0xA998A44B + 0x530C727C = 0xFCA516C7 (L0087).
- step3 = step2 + K[1] = 0xFCA516C7 + 0x71374491 = 0x6DDC5B58 (L0088).
- temp1 = step3 + W[1] = 0x6DDC5B58 + 0x5931492D = 0xC70DA485 (L0089).
- temp2 = S0 + maj = 0xFFEC46B4 + 0x2B4FA6A7 = 0x2B3BED5B (L0092).
- new a = temp1 + temp2 = 0xC70DA485 + 0x2B3BED5B = 0xF24991E0 (L0093).
- new e = d + temp1 = 0x3C6EF372 + 0xC70DA485 = 0x037C97F7 (L0094).

Round 2 (selected checks):
- step1 = h + S1 = 0x9B05688C + 0x9CA6664C = 0x37ABCED8 (L0100).
- step2 = step1 + ch = 0x37ABCED8 + 0x500E53FC = 0x87BA22D4 (L0101).
- step3 = step2 + K[2] = 0x87BA22D4 + 0xB5C0FBCF = 0x3D7B1EA3 (L0102).
- temp1 = step3 + W[2] = 0x3D7B1EA3 + 0x3376766C = 0x70F1950F (L0103).
- temp2 = S0 + maj = 0x95D275FD + 0x6A4981E7 = 0x001BF7E4 (L0106).
- new a = temp1 + temp2 = 0x70F1950F + 0x001BF7E4 = 0x711D8CF3 (L0107).
- new e = d + temp1 = 0xBB67AE85 + 0x70F1950F = 0x2C594394 (L0108).

Mid/late rounds sanity checks (sums only; each matched exactly):
- Round 28: step1, step2, step3, temp1, temp2, new a, new e (L0464–L0473).
- Round 29: step1, step2, step3, temp1, temp2, new a, new e (L0479–L0486).
- Round 35: step1=0x56EC03A3, step2=0xD2A5C865, step3=0x25DDD578, temp1=0xC8602CFE, temp2=0xEB58D6D9, new a=0xB3B903D7, new e=0x59EFA23A (L0562–L0571).
- Round 41: step1=0x2E7A684A, step2=0x5B807A00, step3=0x039B47EB, temp1=0xA25C0D1D, temp2=0x588D1325, new a=0xFB2F2F92, new e=0xE799A6D (L0646–L0654).
- Round 56: step1=0xA5DB30D8, step2=0x5E81BD34, step3=0xD3114022, temp1=0x3080A33A, temp2=0x1B429B62, new a=0x4BC33E9C, new e=0xD3FD068A (L0856–L0864).
- Round 63: step2+K[63]=step3 (0xF97968C3+0xC67178F2=0xBFEAE1B5, L0955–L0957), temp1=0x931DDBC4 (L0957), temp2=0x8D4E747A (L0960), new a=0x206C503E, new e=0xB9EB C057 (L0961–L0962).

Additional small-op checks:
- Round 0 ch (L0071) and maj (L0077) recomputed directly from inputs (L0069) matched exactly (see above).
- Round 0 S1 from e via rotates (L0070) matched my independent recomputation (see above).

Variable rotations/assignments after each round (e.g., L0081, L0095, L0109, L0473, L0571, L0865, L0963) consistently show b←a, c←b, d←c, f←e, g←f, h←g.

Across all these checks:
- Every modular addition line (step1, step2, step3, temp1, temp2, new a, new e) I verified matched exactly the stated operands and sums.
- The small logical operations I reconstructed (S1, ch, maj) for Round 0 matched exactly the bitwise definitions.
- The message/padding mapping to W[0..15] is correct.

Given the single-tamper model (“exactly one intermediate value bit-flipped, with all later values recomputed consistently”), any such flip would have produced at least one mismatch in these per-line equations at the tamper point (e.g., step3 ≠ step2+K, temp1 ≠ step3+W, temp2 ≠ S0+maj, or a small-op mismatch). None were observed across early, mid, and late rounds, nor in the small-op spot checks.

VERDICT: GENUINE
ROUND: NONE
REASONING: Verified message->W[0..15] mapping (L0003–L0018). In Round 0 computed S1 from rotates of e (L0070) and ch, maj (L0071, L0077) directly from inputs (L0069); all matched. For Rounds 0–2, 28–29, 35, 41, 56, 63, re-did all key additions (step1, step2, step3, temp1, temp2, new a, new e; e.g., L0072–L0080, L0086–L0094, L0100–L0108, L0464–L0473, L0562–L0571, L0646–L0654, L0856–L0864, L0955–L0962) and each matched exactly. Variable shifts (e.g., L0081, L0963) are consistent. No inconsistencies found.
